title: Separate raw-SQLite classification state with immutable history
description: Persist rule lifecycle, outcomes, apply recovery, feedback, and cleanup without coupling their retention to Durable Ledger State.
decision: Use one owner-only classify.db under the Tally data root with Microsoft.Data.Sqlite, snake_case tables, foreign keys, WAL, synchronous FULL, bounded busy handling, user_version migrations, explicit transactions, operation idempotency, and guarded single-writer transitions. Immutable versions, outcomes, terminal item results, feedback, and lifecycle rows reject update and delete. Raw corpus rows and full Ledger projections are never persisted.
rationale: Sharing Ledger storage violates the public boundary and couples retention. Flat JSON files make atomic activation, row accounting, migrations, idempotency, and saga recovery fragile. A hosted database violates the self-contained constraint. EF and DbContext add a mapping and migration layer without a second provider or hosted request pipeline. Raw SQLite matches the established Tally storage model and permits exact invariant tests.
